title: Automated Blood Unit Matching based on ABO/Rh Compatibility Rules
description: The system shall automatically match transfusion requests to compatible blood units based on ABO/Rh compatibility rules in real-time.
rationale: To ensure safe and efficient blood allocation without manual cross-checking, reducing the risk of medical errors and improving operational efficiency.
business_value: Improves patient safety by ensuring only compatible blood is administered, thereby reducing potential adverse reactions. Enhances operational efficiency through automated processing.

source.description: As a transfusion service user, I need the system to automatically match transfusion requests to compatible blood units based on ABO/Rh compatibility rules so that I can ensure safe and efficient blood allocation without manual cross-checking.

acceptance_criteria: Given a transfusion request for a patient with a specific ABO/Rh type
When the system processes the request
Then it should only return blood units that are ABO/Rh compatible with the patient
preconditions: The system must have access to up-to-date and accurate ABO/Rh compatibility data for all available blood units
A transfusion request is submitted by a user authorized to make such requests
The system's database of blood units is not empty
postconditions: The system returns only compatible blood units that are safe for the patient to receive
The transfusion request is recorded in the system with details of the matched blood unit(s)
Any incompatible blood units are excluded from consideration
functional_constraints: The matching process must be completed within a maximum response time of 5 seconds
The system must handle at least 100 simultaneous transfusion requests without performance degradation
security_requirements: Access to the ABO/Rh compatibility data must be restricted to authorized personnel only
All data transmitted between the user interface and the backend database must be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher
The system must comply with HIPAA regulations for patient data protection

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system must have a response time of less than 5 seconds for all match requests
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must be able to scale to support up to 10,000 active users and handle 200 concurrent transfusion requests
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages must be clear and actionable, and the user interface should provide real-time feedback on matching status

edge_cases: What happens if a patient has unknown ABO/Rh type?
How does the system handle requests for incompatible blood units?
What if there are insufficient compatible blood units available?
dependencies: Access to up-to-date and accurate ABO/Rh compatibility data

interfaces.name: Blood Unit API
interfaces.description: The system will interact with a Blood Unit API to retrieve and update information about available blood units.

interfaces.name: Transfusion Request Database
interfaces.description: The system will store and manage transfusion requests in this database.

test_cases.description: Verify matching of a request for AB+ blood.
test_cases.expected_result: The system returns only AB+ and ABO/Rh compatible units.

test_cases.description: Verify handling of an unknown ABO/Rh type request.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should return an error message indicating the patient's ABO/Rh type is unknown.
